intro:
There is no information available about the plant Tragacantha calophylla. However, I can provide information on the plant Tragacanth (also known as Tragacantha or Tragacanthus). 

Tragacanth is a type of plant-based adhesive or binder, which is derived from the sap of the Astragalus plant, specifically the species Astragalus gummifer. 

The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a gentle laxative. It has also been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne.

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include complex polysaccharides, specifically galactomannans, which are responsible for its adhesive properties and potential health benefits.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include powders, gels, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and as a gentle laxative.
